name: Student-Simulation Validity Requires Epistemic Fidelity Rather Than Surface Realism
statement: Yuan et al. argue that LLM student simulation should be treated as constrained generation under an explicit Epistemic State Specification, because broadly capable LLMs face a competence paradox when asked to emulate partially knowledgeable learners.
population_or_context: LLM-based student simulation for education research, teacher training, content evaluation, and synthetic data augmentation.
quantitative_note: No empirical results; the paper proposes ESS levels and a reporting card.
caveats: The paper is theoretical and does not evaluate specific models.
It does not provide evidence that any model family is superior.
Its strongest contribution is reporting and validity framing.
reported_metrics: Not applicable; proposes Goal-by-Environment framing and ESS levels E0-E4.

source_locator.section: Abstract; Sections 3.2-3.3; Sections 6.2-6.4; Conclusion; Appendix reporting card
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Extraction uses competence paradox, constrained-generation definition, ESS reporting guidance, goal-aligned fidelity guidance, and limitations.

limitations: Framework evidence should not change empirical confidence in model performance.
Best used to sharpen reporting criteria and avoid model-choice overclaiming.
